---
title: "CV"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.page-header}
::: {.page-shell}
<div class="eyebrow">Curriculum vitae</div>

# Mesfin Genie

Senior Lecturer in Health Economics · Newcastle Business School · The University of Newcastle
:::
:::

::: {.prose}
## Academic appointments

<div class="timeline">
<div class="timeline-item">
<h3>Senior Lecturer in Health Economics</h3>
<p><strong>The University of Newcastle, Australia · 2023–present</strong><br>Newcastle Business School.</p>
</div>
<div class="timeline-item">
<h3>Health Measurement and Regulatory Science Fellow</h3>
<p><strong>Duke University, United States · 2023–2024</strong><br>Department of Population Health Sciences.</p>
</div>
<div class="timeline-item">
<h3>Postdoctoral Research Fellow</h3>
<p><strong>Auburn University, United States · 2022–2023</strong><br>Harrison College of Pharmacy.</p>
</div>
<div class="timeline-item">
<h3>Research Fellow</h3>
<p><strong>University of Aberdeen, United Kingdom · 2020–2022</strong><br>Health Economics Research Unit.</p>
</div>
<div class="timeline-item">
<h3>Postdoctoral Research Fellow</h3>
<p><strong>Ca’ Foscari University of Venice, Italy · 2019–2020</strong><br>Department of Economics.</p>
</div>
</div>

## Education

- **PhD in Economics**, Ca’ Foscari University of Venice, Italy, 2019
- **MSc in Health Economics and Management**, University of Bologna, Italy
- **MA in Economics**, Addis Ababa University, Ethiopia
- **BA in Economics**, Jimma University, Ethiopia

## Research fields

- Health economics and health preference research
- Choice modelling and discrete choice experiments
- Behavioural economics and information processing
- Public health policy and programme evaluation

## Major grants and funded projects

- **MandEval: Effectiveness and consequences of Australia’s COVID-19 vaccine mandates**, Medical Research Future Fund, 2023–2026. Total programme funding approximately A$4.57 million; University of Newcastle allocation A$808,440. Site lead.
- **Upscaling Field Epidemiology Training Programs in India**, World Bank, 2025. A$416,000. Lead.
- University of Newcastle research and conference schemes, 2024–2025.

## Professional service

Guest Editor for <em>Health Policy and Technology</em>. Reviewer for journals including <em>Journal of Health Economics</em>, <em>Health Economics</em>, <em>Social Science & Medicine</em>, <em>Value in Health</em>, <em>Journal of Choice Modelling</em>, <em>Theory and Decision</em> and <em>BMJ Open</em>.

## Contact and profiles

<div class="social-row">
<a class="social-link" href="mailto:mesfin.genie@newcastle.edu.au" title="Email"><i class="bi bi-envelope"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=v2SXM_kAAAAJ&hl=en" title="Google Scholar"><i class="ai ai-google-scholar"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1744-4666" title="ORCID"><i class="ai ai-orcid"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://au.linkedin.com/in/mesfin-genie-ph-d-65634216" title="LinkedIn"><i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://github.com/DrGenie" title="GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
<a class="social-link" href="https://www.newcastle.edu.au/profile/mesfin-genie" title="University profile"><i class="bi bi-building"></i></a>
</div>
:::
